reviews.title: works to add security to fitbit watch band
reviews.text:
Good. I have been using these bands as extra security on my fitbit bands since I first got a fitbit dating back to 2015. I have had several styles since then, and I always make sure to have a pack of these security bands on hand. First it was because the fitbit band was just a pressure close band that easily popped off, then it was a matter of keeping a regular watch band style extra secure. These work on the regular width bands.

When I was ready to buy more of these security bands, I was about to buy some that are made for fitbit, but then I realized that those have a protrusion for locking into the band. I really just wanted something to slip on with no need to make use of the band holes to secure it. These are exactly what I wanted. Nice variety of colors.

reviews.title: I was worried that this cable damaged my watch.
reviews.text: I bought this item as extra charging cables for my Vivomove Style, which has physical watch hands in addition to the electronic display. Every time I use this charger instead of the OEM charger, the time shown by the physical hands is hours off and I have to realign.  It also seems to become much less sensitive to touch until I plug it back into the OEM charger to reset it.  The Vivomove Style will keep the time on the physical hands 2 weeks after the battery dies (and I've never seen it not keep accurate time), so it wasn't a dead battery issue.  I was hoping to have more than one cable to charge my watch but based on the issues I'm having,  I'm going back to the OEM cable.

reviews.title: It could have been so much better.
reviews.text: I have so many feelings about this watch. First off, set up was a breeze. You just open your app store and get the Google app directed. Pairing and synching up your info is very easy. The watch is very beautiful! I was scared the crystals would rub right off like glitter, but I have not had any issues so far. Now for all the bad stuff.. first and foremost the battery life is terrible. I would give it 9-10 hours max. I'm not much of an app person either, so if you are then that means your battery life will be even lower. It has its own charger, so that means you'll have to carry it around with you, especially big your planning to go out after work or school which is very inconvenient. I with it was a standard C USB charger so I can just use my phone charger, but sadly that's not the case. I did an experiment to see how long the battery would last. I charged it to 100%, and left it in my desk right before bed. In the morning at 8 am, I am at 15%. Whoa. Yeah, the battery is just really really terrible. So I thought I'd just change the battery settings..well there is only one other option and that is to go into battery saving mode where all it will display is the time. That in my opinion defeats the purpose of a smart watch. Other than that, I love how I can customize watch faces by using my social media, or pick from a pre made list by Michael Kors. You can also get email notifications, download apps like Google keep to take notes by speaking into your watch, as well as use Google pay, activity tracker, weather, and pause and play music without taking out your phone. So yeah, if your okay with a short battery life, then everything else about this watch is awesome. Just giving you the real tea. Hope you found this review helpful.

reviews.title: Great feminine watch for dress up or down
reviews.text: This watch works with android and iPhone which makes this a winner in my book. It does pretty much everything my iwatch series 4 does and it looks beautiful on my wrist. Charger is magnetic and easy to use.

reviews.title: Easy set up - does what a smartphone can do
reviews.text:
My wife really likes this watch. This is the one with the glittery watchband... It comes with a magnetic charger that just attaches to the back - no fidgeting. And I was surprised at how quickly it charged.

The screen colors are great! Vibrant colors and clear graphics. The touchscreen itself can lag just a little, but overall responds very well. Neither one of us have ever had a smartwatch before and I was a little apprehensive about setting it up, but I downloaded the wear OS app to her phone and from there, it just walked us through the process. It paired on the first try and seems to be working just as it should.

She's now got it set up to check emails and can generally do anything on the internet that she would do with her phone. Then theres the added advantage of the fitness stuff... heart rate monitor, etc.

All in all, she loves it!

reviews.title: Still Searching
reviews.text:
The MKGO has the best battery life of any WearOS watches I've tried. On a fairly busy day with the app running in the background on my iPhone the whole time, I was able to wear the watch from 6 a.m. until I went to bed around 10. The other three android watches I've tried didn't make it to dinner.

But this doesn't have a speaker.

Why doesn't this have a speaker?

One of the indispensable features of my Apple watch is being able to answer a call quickly when I have stepped out of the room my phone is in. I have lupus. I can't run. People somehow seem to know to call about something important when I've gone to the bathroom or stepped out to the mailbox. I'm close enough to be connected to my phone, but not close enough to answer it.

This never happens with spam calls. Just the ones from the people I won't be able to get back to through their nightmare phone menus.

The lack of this feature makes the watch unsuitable for me, and that makes me sad, because the iPhone is a begware spamming disappointment machine these days, and I really want to move to android.

reviews.title: Nice watch
reviews.text: Shipping was very slow but the watch is nice.  Took minutes to install.  easier than my apple watch.  does everything i need that my series 4 does but is discrete and fashionable.  Love the pink color..... my favorite.  Touchscreen works fine and easy to navigate.  A great buy for those who dont necessarily want an apple watch.  I am able to sync my contacts and apps to the watch.  Tracks my heart-rate on command.  I love this watch.

reviews.title: Nice smartwatch
reviews.text: I just got this smartwatch today. The watch face itself is not bulky, it's a little flatter, which I like. I bought this to wear in case I don't have my phone on me or beside me. Not sure how much farther away from my phone I can place a call when I'm home, but be sure to keep the bluetooth on when in use. This watch has many functions, which I have yet to use, but so far it's cute and I like it!

title: Polar Vantage V2 - Premium Multisport Smartwatch with GPS
features: Premium Sleek Design for superior look and feel provides you with a high-performing, lightweight (only 52g) sports watch that won’t weigh you down whilst you train.
Ultra-Long Battery Life - up to 40h of training with full GPS and HR tracking (and even up to 100h with power save options). Up to 7 days in watch mode with 24/7 HR tracking.
Our World-Renowned Optical HR Technology with 10 LED’s for deeper light penetration, and 4 detectors to sense light returning through the blood vessels to track your HR with precision.

The Training Load Pro and Recovery tracking tools help you optimize your training and avoid overtraining. Test your performance and personalize your heart rate, speed and power zones with the running and cycling performance tests.

Fully Automatic and Intelligent Overnight Recovery Measurement applications give you daily feedback on how well you recover from stress and training during the night. This helps you make optimal choices the next day.

reviews.title: Good watch, I recommend
reviews.text: I came from a fitbit for long years and moved to Polar because of stats and data that I wanted! I started off with the Polar Unite since November 2020 and until now I upgraded to the Polar Vantage V2! It's worth it! I love the accuracy of polar. Heart rate, sleep and GPS is super good!

reviews.title: does what it's supposed to
reviews.text:
*edit: I've found a con and it bugged me enough to update my review. If your phone is on "Do not disturb" and you leave your phone on at night for emergency calls, the watch still vibrates with every text, regardless of whether they're on your emergency list. This is annoying when you're a light sleeper and you get a text for go cart racing or Dairy Queen at 4 am. I'm assuming it would still go off with a call as well.

I have a Garmin Vivofit that for some reason the notification vibrations will work for a couple months, then stop working for a while, then randomly start working again. I was sick of this, and went looking for something not too expensive and found this. I'm still getting used to it, but it vibrates when I get a call or text, so I'm happy so far. The band is very flexible and comfortable, which is nice, since my Vivofit band was a little stiff.

reviews.title: Great watch for the price.
reviews.text: After buying the earlier brands of Fitbit which I loved. I bought myself a Luxe on sale for $100 last December 2021. It didn’t record anything accurately and just didn’t function the way it should. CS was if no help. I switched to Amazfit!  This is a great watch, the battery has lasted over a week. It accurately tells time, my heart rate, steps and I love this watch. It’s great and is so light on my wrist!!

reviews.title: Terrible smart watch, inaccurate and waste of time/money
reviews.text: After 5 months of use I’m done with this. It seems to be getting less accurate every workout. It can’t even get step counts correct. The accuracy of the heart beat monitoring varies too. The sleep monitor is often wrong about awake times so I don’t believe the other sleep data either. The only thing I can believe with this watch is the time of day.

reviews.title: Good budget watch if you don't need always-on display
reviews.text: Overall, this is a very reasonably priced watch with a lot of functionality. However, the fact that it doesn't have the always-on screen option was a deal breaker for me and I had to replace it with one of their other models.

reviews.title: Did not like this nearly as much as my original amazfit bip watch
reviews.text:
I bought an Amazfit Bip watch a year or more ago and have really been enjoying it The app tracks all my cycling trips and the sleep monitoring is useful. Iam really a big fan of the long battery life of the older model.

This watch has a 9 day battery life which is not nearly as long as the 30 days of the original watch.

This watch meausres blood oxygen levels but taking the measurement requires holding your wrist it the right position and takes much longer that the finger tip device Ihave.

I did not exhaustively evaluate the new app that came with this phone but it did not look nearly as good as the original app.

title: Garmin Forerunner® 955 Solar, GPS Running Smartwatch with Solar Charging Capabilities, Tailored to Triathletes, Long-Lasting Battery, Whitestone
description: Lead the pack with Forerunner® 955 Solar, the premium GPS running and triathlon smartwatch with Power Glass™ solar charging to extend battery life. The training readiness feature tells you when you’re primed for a productive session — while daily suggested workouts adapt to your performance and recovery alongside the race widget, which provides training tips, course details and completion time predictions. With morning report, you can get an overview of your sleep, recovery, training and more — as well as a picture of your overall health with heart rate variability (HRV) status (This device is intended to give an estimate of your activity and metrics). On the run, new multi-band GPS and full-color, built-in maps help you navigate confidently. And with traditional buttons and a touch display, it has never been easier to tap into your training status and know whether you’re training productively, peaking or strained. If you’re pushing toward the podium — or to the very limits — Forerunner 955 Solar is by your side with up to 20 days of battery life with solar charging in smartwatch mode (assuming all-day wear with 3 hours per day outside in 50,000 lux conditions).
features: Built with an always-on, full-color display that’s light on the wrist and easy to read even in direct sunlight

Battery life: up to 20 days of battery life in smartwatch mode for a full picture of your health — from sleep to training (assuming all-day wear with 3 hours per day outside in 50,000 lux conditions); up to 42 hours in GPS mode and up to 49 hours with solar charging (assuming continuous use for entire period in 50,000 lux conditions); up to 110 hours in UltraTrac mode with solar charging (assuming use in 50,000 lux conditions)
Click or swipe through maps and stats with traditional button controls or a new, responsive touchscreen
Whether you’re running on city streets or riding densely covered trails, Forerunner 955 provides full-color, built-in mapping to keep you on track

Access multiple global navigation satellite systems, and get access to multiple frequencies sent by navigation satellites for improved accuracy in areas where GNSS signals are reflected, weak or typically don't penetrate — giving you superior accuracy for how far and how fast you’ve run — even in challenging environments
From the moment you wake up, get a training readiness score based on your sleep quality, recovery, training and more, so you can determine whether it’s a good day to go hard — or take it easy
Get ready for your next race with race widget — which provides training tips, personalized daily suggested workouts and completion time predictions based on course details, weather and performance

Using new indicators such as HRV status, your recent exercise history and performance, get an easy insight into your overall effort with training status — and know whether you’re training productively, peaking or strained

Receive an overview of your sleep, recovery and training outlook as soon as you wake up with morning report — alongside HRV status, training readiness and weather; you can even customize your report to show what you want to see (This device is intended to give an estimate of your activity and metrics)

With HRV status, gain a deeper understanding of your overall health, recovery and training performance through heart rate variability while you sleep, based on technology developed by our Firstbeat Analytics team (This device is intended to give an estimate of your activity and metrics)

reviews.title: A great watch for a runner
reviews.text: Haven't found anything I don't like about this watch.  Have begun following it's training advice, and it appears to be working.  Provides a wealth of interesting information.  One real plus for us old guys: the face is larger than the 735XT making it easier for these old eyes to read it.

reviews.title: Buena compra
reviews.text: Llego excelente, bien empacado

reviews.title: Resumen de datos y métricas de seguimiento
reviews.text: Verdaderamente útil, el seguimiento de sueño, entreno, potencia, y muchos mas datos que nos permiten dar un seguimiento más preciso del estado físico.

reviews.title: VO2 and race prediction are garbage
reviews.text:
For some reason with the 955 - Garmin decided to stop updating VO2 and race prediction - so if you have come to rely/look at that - don't get this watch. They have tried fixing it with the last few beta updates, but they can't seem to figure out how to correct it.

Multi-band GPS is nice - which honestly is the only reason we all haven't moved back to the 945/735 - which are AMAZING watches.

reviews.title: Fantastic update
reviews.text:
The 955 is a fantastic update over the previous 9XX, 7XX, and 6XX models. This update I'd much more communicative and provides loads more encouraging updates and workout suggestions. The interface is also more fluid than previous models. The return of touch to the user interface is nice but, optional.

Definitely worth going with

reviews.title: My Approach S6 -- Near Perfection!
reviews.text: Very pleased with the quality of this band replacement for my Approach S6 band and the ease of installation. Having a buddy helps, but the installation is easily accomplished on your own within a matter of minutes.  The new band is more flexible than the original which, IMHO, is a plus.  What I can't say is that the original band might have stiffened with age.  Who knows and it doesn't matter.  The screwdrivers are also an awesome design -- the handle ends have a hex shape you can stick in your drill if your hands are weak.  The only minuscule minus is that the retainer bar/strap is a tad large as others have stated, but problem solved by reusing the retainer strap from the original band. VERY pleased with this purchase.

Bluetooth smart watches have a built-in microphone and speaker, so you can easily answer and make calls and receive notifications from your phone and apps by simply connecting to your phone once via bluetooth. Tap bluetooth fitness watch's voice wake function to play music while you're exercising, working out.

🌈【Multi-Sport Modes and IP67 Wateproof】Fitness watch supports 11 sport modes,fitness tracker watch accurately records all-day activities (steps, distance, calories and active minutes), motivating you to reach your exercise goal with real-time statistics on the fitness smartwatch. IP67 waterproof smartwatch，you can wear it when washing hands or running in the rain with it.You don't have to worry about short trips away from home, sports smartwatch can support 3-5 days of use.

🌈【All-Round Manage Your Health】: The smartwatch health tracker can detect your 24/7 heart rate, blood pressure and blood oxygen to get a better understanding of your health (Not for medical use). The high-performance motion sensor monitors sleep status automatically and provides comprehensive sleep quality analysis (deep sleep, light sleep and awake).And a breathing function,you can keep a good mind with the health smartwatch to go for a healthier body.

🌈【Wider Compatibility and Multifunction】The outdoor smartwatch is compatible with most iOS 8.2 & Android 5.1 or higher smart phones version. Fitness activity tracker watch includes more convenient life features,such as split screen function,music & camera control, 5 levels of brightness adjustment, bright screen time,weather display, sedentary & drink water reminder, flashlight, calendar, stopwatch, calculator, Alarm Clock, Do Not Disturb/Power-saving Mode, Find watch.

reviews.title: Seems nice
reviews.text:
Seems like a nice watch. It has a standard user interface for a smart watch.

I like the design and think it’s nice. Seems to have the basic functionality you’d expect for a smart watch and using it for workouts

I like the design and think that it seems nice

reviews.title: Works well for step counting and can measure heartrate upon request. Otherwise less than stellar
reviews.text:
This generally works well enough for exercise purposes. I didn't get the "dangerous app warning" on my iPhone, though I did get a warning that if I use the watch to display messages, then the app has access to all my contacts and the text of all my messages. I doubt I'm a target for Chinese spying so I chose to ignore that, but it's still worth noting.

I love that it's waterproof, other watch faces are available (though I couldn't figure out how to restore the original, more-helpful one without doing a full factory reset), it tracks steps and sleep, plus it's able to measure heartrate and blood pressure. Mine sometimes tells me the weather, but only immediately after connecting to my Google account on my phone; otherwise it just says "unknown" (and honestly, if I have to click extra buttons on my phone, I can just check the weather there). It has a music playback control, but I couldn't get that to work. It also theoretically connects to my phone and lets me enter a phone number, but I couldn't click "dial" so that bit felt more like a child's toy, where you pretend to call a phone but don't actually do it. But since I use this for exercise more than connectivity, I'm OK with that.

Some things are less than stellar, like their heart rate screen always shows a default chart (I don't think it ever lets you create your own heart rate chart)... and it shows a heartrate from 120-150 ALWAYS, which catches me off guard when I've been sitting at my desk for 2 hours. But I learned it's just a concept; there is no real *graph of my heartrate that I can find, just current / min / max that I can see from my phone. Or I can click "measure" to get my instant heartrate on my phone (good while exercising). Blood pressure can sometimes be measured from your phone (though it takes 1-4 minutes for some reason), and sometimes that screen disappears completely; I haven't yet figured out the pattern

It doesn't seem to measure many things other than steps automatically; you have to select each one when you're wanting to see it. Soooooooo all told it's solidly mediocre. It's good for counting steps, manually checking heartrate, and manually checking blood pressure (very slowly) on occasion; otherwise it's a lot weaker than most smart phones

title: DR.VIVA Smart Watches for Men, Fitness Tracker with Heart Rate Monitor, 1.3" Full Touch Screen Sleep Monitor 18 Sport Modes 7-Day Battery Life 5ATM Waterproof Watches for iPhone/Android Phones
features: 【Heart Rate Monitor & Sleep Monitor】Built-in PPG heart rate sensor coupled with HR algorithm, the smart watch can accurately monitor your heart rate during wearing. Automatic sleep tracking can monitor your sleep status (deep sleep, shallow sleep, awakening) and provide a comprehensive sleep quality analysis. All health tracking metrics can help you get a better understanding of your health and make reasonable adjustments to your lifestyle.

【Accurate Fitness Tracker & 18 Sport Modes】The activity tracker accurately record all-day activities like steps, distance, calories burned, miles walked, active minutes. The fitness tracker supports 18 sports modes: running, walking, climbing, cycling, basketball, football, tennis, aerobics, yoga, rope jumping, sit-ups, swimming, etc. Can give you more detailed insights into your whole day's activities and improve your athletic performance. Stay motivated all day!

【Long Battery Life & 5ATM Waterproof】The fitness tracker for men is equipped with a 170mAh Li-Polymer battery, only takes 2 hours to get fully charged by a magnetic charging cable to support 7 days of normal use. The fitness watch is 5ATM waterproof, you can wear it when washing hands, in the rain, or swimming in the shallow water, which allows you to swim in the pool with it without worries. (Note: Do Not support hot water shower, diving, surfing, saunas, spring, etc).

【1.3" Full Touch Screen & Custom Watch Face】Our smart watch with 1.3 inch IPS LCD color touch screen display, brings a resolution of 240x240. You can also customize the watch face in the "Zeroner" App to show your personality. DIY for your preferences and mood.

【Smart Notifications & More Practical Tools】The smartwatch is wide compatibility with iOS & Android. You can receive incoming calls or read SMS and SNS notifications on your smartwatch. (It CAN'T answer/reply to messages, calls, or text back.) (Including SMS, Facebook, Whatsapp, Twitter, Instagram, etc.). You will never miss important news. The smartwatches also feature remote control of music and camera, sedentary reminder, alarm clock, stopwatch, timer, a compass to meet your daily needs.

reviews.title: Keep track
reviews.text: This fitness tracker is simple to setup and in no time it'll be keeping track of your health so you have a monitor of what you do every day. It's simple to track and manage your sleep patterns or steps you take in a day. Very simple to use mobile app. The watch lasts a long time before dieing at least 2 days

reviews.title: Super impressed with this watch. Truly remarkable
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:ae47b9651b52b2c1b0485219f33680bf]] Wow. I am not impressed by much, but this watch is truly impressive for this price point.

Build: Very good build quality. The waterproof case feels solid, with responsive buttons and full color high res screen. Bracelet is soft rubber and is very comfortable. It's a very large band, so large or small wrists will fit in here.

Features: Though not perfect, the features on this watch are impressive. Measures steps, activities, heart rate, laps, sleep quality. Very very nice featuers. Plus it's waterproof!!!

Cons: Some of the interfaces are a bit clunky, and the app could use work, but it's fairly intuitive, and easy to use. I connected to my Galaxy S7 with no problems.

I recommend this watch!

reviews.title: My favorite budget smart watch
reviews.text:
I've tried a variety of budget smart watches, and this one by Dr.Viva has become my favorite. It's very comfortable, great flexible band, no issues with feeling like it's pressing into my wrist bones. The screen display is a good size without being too large, perfect for smaller wrists.

The Zeroner app has been fantastic, no problems. It accurately tracks my steps, sleep & oxygen. I like how it has a variety of exercises for me to document. The battery life is a big winner. I recharge the smart watch once a week, compared to twice a week with the other watches I've tried before. I was disappointed that I don't have the option to have my own picture as the screen display, but the app still has plenty of screens to choose from.

If you're like me and don't want to spend an exorbitant price on a smart watch, but would still like to enjoy the features of a smart watch this one would be a great option.

reviews.title: Inaccurate
reviews.text: This is a cute watch. Set up was simple. I liked the pink band and it was comfortable. The multi-lingual instructions are useless. Even worse it is inaccurate. I was at my desk and took it off. When I came back it said I logged 1800 steps.  It was sitting in the same place on the desk. Not. Back to my fit bit.

reviews.title: Very Durable and Love the Face
reviews.text:
The watch and band are more durable than I expected at this price point. The watch is very easy to install and should not take more than 5 minutes. For some reason I really like the aesthetics on the face of the watch.

I have another watch that is comparable and this watch costs 25% less. This is definitely good value. A watch like this is a game changer for people monitoring heart issues. Highly recommend.
